per_turn_leaks.student_message: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
I think what happens during these episodes is that they usually start with a sharp pain in my right side of my chest. It's like a stabbing sensation, and it worsens when I breathe in deeply or cough. Sometimes, this pain is accompanied by a wheezing sound, which freaks me out because I've never had anything like that before.

As the episode progresses, I start to feel short of breath, and my heart rate increases. It feels like my body is struggling to get enough oxygen, even though I'm trying to breathe normally. The coughing gets worse, and it's like my whole chest is constricting. It's really hard to describe, but it's a feeling of intense pressure that builds up in my chest.

Eventually, the pain subsides, and the wheezing slows down, but not before I'm left feeling completely drained and exhausted. It's like my body has been put through a wringer, and I just want to collapse from exhaustion.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, yes. Sometimes during these episodes, I'll get really hot and sweaty all over, like my body is overheating or something. It's a weird feeling, but it's definitely happened a few times when I've had these intense chest pain and wheezing episodes. And sometimes, after it's all over, I feel really thirsty and my mouth feels dry, even though I've been drinking plenty of water.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yes. During those intense episodes, I do get pretty sweaty. It's not just a little bit of sweat, either – it's like my whole body is soaking through my clothes. And sometimes, after it's all over, my skin can feel really flushed and warm to the touch.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I have been experiencing low-grade fever, usually around 38.2°C (100.8°F), which tends to be worse in the evenings. And yes, I do get night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for about 4 nights now. It's really uncomfortable and disrupts my sleep even more.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually been experiencing some weight loss over the past week or so. It's not drastic, but I can tell my clothes are fitting a bit looser than usual. As for appetite, it's definitely been affected by this illness. I've had days where I just don't feel like eating anything at all, and even when I do try to eat, nothing seems to taste right or fill me up.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
My mom has asthma, which is something I've always been aware of since I was young. Other than that, I don't know if there's anything else in my family history that would be relevant to what I'm experiencing right now. My grandmother did pass away from lung cancer, but that was a long time ago when she was much older.

As for other medical conditions or chronic illnesses in the family, I don't think there's anything significant that comes to mind. But like I said, my mom has asthma, and sometimes I'll notice that she gets these wheezing episodes too, especially during her allergy season. It's always been something we've talked about, but it hasn't really affected me directly until now.

I'm also allergic to penicillin – whenever I take it, I break out in a rash. But that's the only medication-related issue I've ever had. Otherwise, my family history is pretty clean as far as medical conditions go.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
My mom's asthma is probably the most relevant condition when it comes to my own health issues. She's always been very open with me about her symptoms and how she manages them, so I've learned a lot from her over the years. But aside from that, I don't think there's anything else in our family history that would be directly related to what I'm experiencing now.

Actually, it might be worth mentioning that my mom has mentioned some concerns about her own health lately – she's been having some issues with her joints and has been feeling a bit more fatigued than usual. We've talked about the possibility of her developing an autoimmune disorder, but nothing has been officially diagnosed yet. I'm not sure if this is relevant to what I'm going through, but it might be worth exploring further.

As for other family members, my grandmother's passing from lung cancer does give me some concern, especially since I've been experiencing these respiratory issues myself. But again, that was a long time ago and she was much older when she passed away, so it's hard to say if there's any direct connection to what I'm dealing with now.

I guess the most important thing for me is to figure out what's causing my symptoms and how to manage them effectively. My family history might not be super relevant in this case, but it's always good to know where we come from and whether there are any underlying conditions that could be contributing to my health issues.
